div1.entryFree:
Dispensation, 1) exemption from law, granting of a license: a d. may be had, H6A V, 3, 86.
2) a tampering with sth., a specious pretence for evading a duty, a plausible excuse: and with good thoughts makes d., urging the worser sense for vantage still, Lucr. 248. he rather means to lodge you in the field, than seek a d. for his oath, LLL II, 87.
